heading: Advanced manufacturing production credit
label: 45X Advanced manufacturing production credit
notes: Amendment of Subsection (d)(4), (f), , , 276, provided that, applicable to components sold during taxable years beginning after , subsection (d)(4) of this section is amended to read as follows:Pub. L. 119–21, title VII, § 70514(a)139 Stat. 273July 4, 2025Dec. 31, 2026(4) Sale of integrated components(A) In generalFor purposes of this section, a person shall be treated as having sold an eligible component to an unrelated person if—(i) such component (referred to in this paragraph as the “primary component”) is integrated, incorporated, or assembled into another eligible component (referred to in this paragraph as the “secondary component”) produced within the same manufacturing facility as the primary component, and(ii) the secondary component is sold to an unrelated person.(B) Additional requirementsSubparagraph (A) shall only apply with respect to a secondary component for which not less than 65 percent of the total direct material costs which are paid or incurred (within the meaning of section 461 and any regulations issued under section 263A) by the taxpayer to produce such secondary component are attributable to primary components which are mined, produced, or manufactured in the United States.See 2025 Amendment note below.

References in TextThe date of the enactment of this section, referred to in subsec. (c)(1)(B), is the date of enactment of , which was approved .Pub. L. 117–169Aug. 16, 2022The date of enactment of this subparagraph, referred to in subsec. (c)(1)(C), is the date of enactment of , which was approved .Pub. L. 119–21July 4, 2025

children.label: A
children.text: produced by the taxpayer, and

children.label: B
children.text: during the taxable year, sold by such taxpayer to an unrelated person.

children.heading: Production and sale must be in trade or business
children.label: 2 Production and sale must be in trade or business
children.text: Any eligible component produced and sold by the taxpayer shall be taken into account only if the production and sale described in paragraph (1) is in a trade or business of the taxpayer.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, a taxpayer shall be treated as selling components to an unrelated person if such component is sold to such person by a person related to the taxpayer.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: At the election of the taxpayer (in such form and manner as the Secretary may prescribe), a sale of components by such taxpayer to a related person shall be deemed to have been made to an unrelated person.

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: ii Requirement
children.text: As a condition of, and prior to, any election described in clause (i), the Secretary may require such information or registration as the Secretary deems necessary for purposes of preventing duplication, fraud, or any improper or excessive amount determined under paragraph (1).

children.text: in the case of any applicable critical mineral, an amount equal to 10 percent (2.5 percent in the case of metallurgical coal) of the costs incurred by the taxpayer with respect to production of such mineral.

children.heading: Phase out for applicable critical minerals other than metallurgical coal
children.label: C Phase out for applicable critical minerals other than metallurgical coal

children.heading: Termination for wind energy components
children.label: D Termination for wind energy components
children.text: This section shall not apply to any wind energy component produced and sold after .December 31, 2027

children.heading: Termination for metallurgical coal
children.label: E Termination for metallurgical coal
children.text: This section shall not apply to any metallurgical coal produced after .December 31, 2029

children.heading: Limitation on capacity of battery cells and battery modules
children.label: 4 Limitation on capacity of battery cells and battery modules

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (K)(ii) or (L)(ii) of paragraph (1), the capacity determined under either subparagraph with respect to a battery cell or battery module shall not exceed a capacity-to-power ratio of 100:1.

children.heading: Capacity-to-power ratio
children.label: B Capacity-to-power ratio
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “capacity-to-power ratio” means, with respect to a battery cell or battery module, the ratio of the capacity of such cell or module to the maximum discharge amount of such cell or module.

children.label: i
children.text: any solar energy component,

children.label: ii
children.text: any wind energy component,

children.label: iii
children.text: any inverter described in subparagraphs (B) through (G) of paragraph (2),

children.label: iv
children.text: any qualifying battery component, and

children.label: v
children.text: any applicable critical mineral.

children.heading: Application with other credits
children.label: B Application with other credits
children.text: The term “eligible component” shall not include any property which is produced at a facility if the basis of any property which is part of such facility is taken into account for purposes of the credit allowed under section 48C after the date of the enactment of this section.

children.heading: Material assistance from prohibited foreign entities
children.label: C Material assistance from prohibited foreign entities
children.text: In the case of taxable years beginning after the date of enactment of this subparagraph, the term “eligible component” shall not include any property which includes any material assistance from a prohibited foreign entity (as defined in section 7701(a)(52), as applied by substituting “used in a product sold before ” for “used in a product sold before ” in subparagraph (D)(iv)(II)(bb) thereof).January 1, 2027January 1, 2030

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “inverter” means an end product which is suitable to convert direct current electricity from 1 or more solar modules or certified distributed wind energy systems into alternating current electricity.

children.heading: Central inverter
children.label: B Central inverter
children.text: The term “central inverter” means an inverter which is suitable for large utility-scale systems and has a capacity which is greater than 1,000 kilowatts (expressed on a per alternating current watt basis).

children.label: i
children.text: is suitable for commercial or utility-scale applications,

children.label: ii
children.text: has a rated output of 208, 480, 600, or 800 volt three-phase power, and

children.label: iii
children.text: has a capacity which is not less than 20 kilowatts and not greater than 125 kilowatts (expressed on a per alternating current watt basis).

children.label: I
children.text: is used in a residential or non-residential system which utilizes 1 or more certified distributed wind energy systems, and

children.label: II
children.text: has a rated output of not greater than 150 kilowatts.

children.heading: Certified distributed wind energy system
children.label: ii Certified distributed wind energy system
children.text: The term “certified distributed wind energy system” means a wind energy system which is certified by an accredited certification agency to meet Standard 9.1-2009 of the American Wind Energy Association (including any subsequent revisions to or modifications of such Standard which have been approved by the American National Standards Institute).

children.label: i
children.text: is suitable to connect with one solar module,

children.label: I
children.text: 120 or 240 volt single-phase power, or

children.label: II
children.text: 208 or 480 volt three-phase power, and

children.label: iii
children.text: has a capacity which is not greater than 650 watts (expressed on a per alternating current watt basis).

children.label: i
children.text: is suitable for a residence,

children.label: ii
children.text: has a rated output of 120 or 240 volt single-phase power, and

children.label: iii
children.text: has a capacity which is not greater than 20 kilowatts (expressed on a per alternating current watt basis).

children.label: i
children.text: is suitable for commercial or utility-scale systems,

children.label: ii
children.text: has a rated output of not less than 600 volt three-phase power, and

children.label: iii
children.text: has a capacity which is greater than 125 kilowatts and not greater than 1000 kilowatts (expressed on a per alternating current watt basis)1So in original. Probably should be followed by a period.1

children.label: i
children.text: Solar modules.

children.label: ii
children.text: Photovoltaic cells.

children.label: iii
children.text: Photovoltaic wafers.

children.label: iv
children.text: Solar grade polysilicon.

children.label: v
children.text: Torque tubes or structural fasteners.

children.label: vi
children.text: Polymeric backsheets.

children.heading: Photovoltaic cell
children.label: i Photovoltaic cell
children.text: The term “photovoltaic cell” means the smallest semiconductor element of a solar module which performs the immediate conversion of light into electricity.

children.label: aa
children.text: directly from molten or evaporated solar grade polysilicon or deposition of solar grade thin film semiconductor photon absorber layer, or

children.label: bb
children.text: through formation of an ingot from molten polysilicon and subsequent slicing, and

children.label: II
children.text: which comprises the substrate or absorber layer of one or more photovoltaic cells.

children.heading: Polymeric backsheet
children.label: iii Polymeric backsheet
children.text: The term “polymeric backsheet” means a sheet on the back of a solar module which acts as an electric insulator and protects the inner components of such module from the surrounding environment.

children.label: I
children.text: suitable for use in photovoltaic manufacturing, and

children.label: II
children.text: purified to a minimum purity of 99.999999 percent silicon by mass.

children.label: I
children.text: suitable to generate electricity when exposed to sunlight, and

children.label: II
children.text: ready for installation without an additional manufacturing process.

children.heading: Solar tracker
children.label: vi Solar tracker
children.text: The term “solar tracker” means a mechanical system that moves solar modules according to the position of the sun and to increase energy output.

children.label: aa
children.text: is part of a solar tracker,

children.label: bb
children.text: is of any cross-sectional shape,

children.label: cc
children.text: may be assembled from individually manufactured segments,

children.label: dd
children.text: spans longitudinally between foundation posts,

children.label: ee
children.text: supports solar panels and is connected to a mounting attachment for solar panels (with or without separate module interface rails), and

children.label: ff
children.text: is rotated by means of a drive system.

children.label: aa
children.text: to connect the mechanical and drive system components of a solar tracker to the foundation of such solar tracker,

children.label: bb
children.text: to connect torque tubes to drive assemblies, or

children.label: cc
children.text: to connect segments of torque tubes to one another.

children.label: i
children.text: Blades.

children.label: ii
children.text: Nacelles.

children.label: iii
children.text: Towers.

children.label: iv
children.text: Offshore wind foundations.

children.label: v
children.text: Related offshore wind vessels.

children.heading: Blade
children.label: i Blade
children.text: The term “blade” means an airfoil-shaped blade which is responsible for converting wind energy to low-speed rotational energy.

children.label: I
children.text: fixed platforms, such as offshore wind monopiles, jackets, or gravity-based foundations, or

children.label: II
children.text: floating platforms and associated mooring systems.

children.heading: Nacelle
children.label: iii Nacelle
children.text: The term “nacelle” means the assembly of the drivetrain and other tower-top components of a wind turbine (with the exception of the blades and the hub) within their cover housing.

children.heading: Related offshore wind vessel
children.label: iv Related offshore wind vessel
children.text: The term “related offshore wind vessel” means any vessel which is purpose-built or retrofitted for purposes of the development, transport, installation, operation, or maintenance of offshore wind energy components.

children.heading: Tower
children.label: v Tower
children.text: The term “tower” means a tubular or lattice structure which supports the nacelle and rotor of a wind turbine.

children.label: i
children.text: Electrode active materials.

children.label: ii
children.text: Battery cells.

children.label: iii
children.text: Battery modules.

children.heading: Electrode active material
children.label: i Electrode active material
children.text: The term “electrode active material” means cathode materials, anode materials, anode foils, and electrochemically active materials, including solvents, additives, and electrolyte salts that contribute to the electrochemical processes necessary for energy storage.

children.label: I
children.text: comprised of 1 or more positive electrodes and 1 or more negative electrodes,

children.label: II
children.text: with an energy density of not less than 100 watt-hours per liter, and

children.label: III
children.text: capable of storing at least 12 watt-hours of energy.

children.label: aa
children.text: in the case of a module using battery cells, with 2 or more battery cells which are configured electrically, in series or parallel, to create voltage or current, as appropriate, to a specified end use, or

children.label: bb
children.text: with no battery cells,

children.label: II
children.text: with an aggregate capacity of not less than 7 kilowatt-hours (or, in the case of a module for a hydrogen fuel cell vehicle, not less than 1 kilowatt-hour), and

children.label: III
children.text: which is comprised of all other essential equipment needed for battery functionality, such as current collector assemblies and voltage sense harnesses, or any other essential energy collection equipment.

children.text: Graphite which is purified to a minimum purity of 99.9 percent graphitic carbon by mass.

Reg. 22711 ())), regardless of whether such production occurs inside or outside of the United States.May 29, 2025

children.heading: Related persons
children.label: 1 Related persons
children.text: Persons shall be treated as related to each other if such persons would be treated as a single employer under the regulations prescribed under section 52(b).

children.heading: Only production in the United States taken into account
children.label: 2 Only production in the United States taken into account
A
children.text: the United States (within the meaning of section 638(1)), or

children.label: B
children.text: a possession of the United States (within the meaning of section 638(2)).

children.heading: Pass-thru in the case of estates and trusts
children.label: 3 Pass-thru in the case of estates and trusts
children.text: Under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, rules similar to the rules of subsection (d) of section 52 shall apply.

children.heading: Sale of integrated components3So in original. There are two pars. (4).3
children.label: 4 Sale of integrated components3So in original. There are two pars. (4).3
children.text: For purposes of this section, a person shall be treated as having sold an eligible component to an unrelated person if such component is integrated, incorporated, or assembled into another eligible component which is sold to an unrelated person.

children.heading: Restrictions relating to prohibited foreign entities3
children.label: 4 Restrictions relating to prohibited foreign entities3
i
children.text: a specified foreign entity (as defined in section 7701(a)(51)(B)), or

children.label: ii
children.text: a foreign-influenced entity (as defined in section 7701(a)(51)(D), without regard to clause (i)(II) thereof).

children.heading: Effective control
children.label: B Effective control
children.text: In the case of a taxpayer for which section 7701(a)(51)(D)(i)(II) is determined to apply for any taxable year, no credit shall be determined under subsection (a) for such taxable year if such determination relates to an eligible component described in subsection (c)(1).
